summary: FQ-13P1 consumes the enemy variant pools that FQ-13P0 found AVAILABLE_NOT_CONSUMED. simple_threat._select_sprite (run once in _ready) prefers a deterministic variant from visual_variant_textures('enemies', enemy_id), else the canonical visual_texture, else the code-drawn body; the choice is fixed for the enemy's life and never reselected during damage, movement, pause, or redraw (_draw still reads the single _art). variant_for(id, cell, seed, pool_size) = posmod(hash('id:cell:seed'), pool_size) mirrors the block variant rule, with enemy_id salting the hash so different kinds sharing a cell can differ; it returns -1 for an empty pool, and a new variant_index field records the pick. The selection is presentation-only and never saved: serialize_threats already stores position + enemy_id, so a restored enemy recomputes the identical variant from the same cell + seed. asset_audit.py adds 'enemies' to VARIANT_CONSUMERS (audit now 0 findings, 0 data bugs); UI_ASSET_GAPS.md marks enemy variants LIVE and records the operator-confirmed full-body-pool player-variation decision. Five fq13p1_* smoke checks cover pool discovery, >1 variant across inputs, determinism, lifetime stability through a hit + redraw + physics frames, and the code-drawn fallback for a poolless/canonical-less enemy. Suite 283 -> 288. The Node2D-typed-world inference trap that hung FQ-12/FQ-13 was avoided by explicitly typing the world.cell_of/world.world_seed reads.
scope: FQ-13P1 per the FQ-13P arc - wire deterministic, lifetime-stable enemy sprite variant selection with a clean pool -> canonical -> code-drawn fallback. No gameplay math, no save-schema change, no new art authored.
next_action: FQ-13P2-P4: generated UI/orb/slot placeholders for the HUD redesign, the player full-body cosmetic pool (design locked), and the opening variant-vs-animation-frame distinction. Then FQ-14.
